skill.name: medical-cv-resume-builder
skill.description: Generates structured medical CVs and resumes in markdown format following US academic standards. Formats education history and professional experience into clean, organized documents suitable for residency applications, grant submissions, and academic portfolios.
skill.summary: Build professional medical CVs and resumes with structured sections for education and experience.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 38 static analyzer findings were evaluated as false positives. The backtick detections are markdown code formatting, not Ruby shell execution. The path traversal detections appear in a security checklist that prohibits such patterns. Hardcoded URL at line 7 is a source attribution link, not a network call. The Python script (scripts/main.py) performs only in-memory string formatting with no network, filesystem, or external command execution. No real security risks were identified after contextual evaluation.

content.user_title: Build Professional Medical CVs and Resumes
content.value_statement: Creating a medical CV that meets US academic standards is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill generates properly formatted medical CVs and resumes with structured sections, so you can focus on your research instead of document formatting.
actual_capabilities: Generate medical CVs in markdown format with structured Education and Experience sections
Format work experiences and education history into US-standard medical CV layout
Support both CV and resume output types based on user preference
Execute via a packaged Python script with reproducible, deterministic output
Validate inputs before processing to prevent malformed output
limitations: Limited to Education and Experience sections only — does not include publications, grants, or certifications
Output is markdown only — does not generate PDF, DOCX, or LaTeX formats
Uses hardcoded example data when run standalone — requires user to modify CONFIG for custom input
No template customization options beyond CV vs resume type selection

use_cases.title: Medical Student Building First CV
use_cases.description: A medical student needs to create their first academic CV for residency applications. The skill formats their education and clinical experience into a clean, structured document.

use_cases.title: Physician Updating Career Documents
use_cases.description: An experienced physician needs to update their CV with recent positions and achievements. The skill organizes their work history into a professional US-standard format.

use_cases.title: Academic Researcher Formatting Grant CV
use_cases.description: A medical researcher needs a properly formatted CV for a grant application. The skill ensures their academic credentials and research experience follow standard conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Basic CV Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a medical CV with my education: [list your degrees] and experience: [list your positions].
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user creating a simple CV

prompt_templates.title: Resume Mode
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a concise medical resume, not a full CV, with these experiences: [list experiences] and this education: [list education]. Focus on recent positions only.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs a shorter resume instead of full CV

prompt_templates.title: Custom Section Order
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a medical CV prioritizing my clinical experience first: [list clinical roles], then research: [list research positions], and finally education: [list degrees].

prompt_templates.title: Batch CV Update
prompt_templates.prompt: Update my existing CV by adding these new positions: [list new roles] to my current experience section, while keeping my education section unchanged: [list existing education].
prompt_templates.scenario: User updating an existing CV with new entries

output_examples.input: Create a CV with MD from Harvard and residency at Mass General
output_examples.output: A structured Curriculum Vitae with a clear EDUCATION section listing the MD degree first, followed by an EXPERIENCE section detailing the residency position with dates. The document uses markdown headings and bullet points for clean, professional presentation.

output_examples.input: Build a resume with BS Biology and Research Fellow experience
output_examples.output: A concise resume format opening with EDUCATION showing the BS in Biology, then EXPERIENCE highlighting the Research Fellow role. The output is shorter than a full CV, focusing on the most relevant recent positions in a scannable layout.

output_examples.input: Generate a medical CV with fellowship, residency, and dual degrees
output_examples.output: A comprehensive CV with EDUCATION listing both degrees in reverse chronological order, followed by EXPERIENCE organized with the fellowship first, then residency. Each entry includes the role title and date range in a consistent format suitable for academic applications.

best_practices: Provide complete education and experience details including dates and institution names for best results
Review the generated markdown and adjust formatting manually before submitting to applications
Specify cv or resume type upfront to get the appropriate level of detail
anti_patterns: Do not submit the raw markdown output directly to applications without reviewing and customizing it first
Do not expect the skill to generate publication lists, award sections, or other specialized CV categories
Do not run the script with empty or malformed input lists — the builder needs structured data to produce useful output

faq.question: What format does the CV output use?
faq.answer: The skill outputs CVs in markdown format with clear section headings and bullet-point entries for education and experience.

faq.question: Can I add more sections like publications or awards?
faq.answer: The current version supports Education and Experience sections only. Additional sections require modifying the Python script.

faq.question: How do I provide my own data instead of the examples?
faq.answer: Edit the example lists in the scripts/main.py file or pass your data when calling the build function directly.

faq.question: Does this work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code for generating and formatting medical CVs.

faq.question: What is the difference between CV and resume mode?
faq.answer: CV mode produces a comprehensive academic document with full history, while resume mode creates a shorter summary focused on recent positions.

faq.question: Can the skill generate PDF or Word documents?
faq.answer: No, the output is markdown only. You can convert the markdown to PDF or DOCX using a separate conversion tool.
